Returns: 0 - on success 1 - he is already there -1 - he still does not exist (didn't get memory) =====================================================================

name: UserDel
reason: doc says it destroys all the user's permissions and detaches him; ends lifetime of the whole user object

cfr.name: UserDel
cfr.documentation: UserDel- Remove a user from the list. Destroy all his permissions and completely detach him from the session. Returns 0 - success -1 - he who does not exist cannot be removed =====================================================================

name: UserFreeCopyBuffer

cfr.name: UserFreeCopyBuffer

name: main
reason: Program entry point; allocations are incidental to startup.

reason: Macro that frees the pointer and nulls it; a deliberate free wrapper.

name: FindSocket
reason: Searches for matching sockets and returns counts; alloc/free are incidental.

cfr.name: FindSocket
cfr.documentation: The first good socket is stored in fdp and its name is appended to SocketPath. If none exists or fdp is NULL SocketPath is not changed. Returns: number of good sockets.

name: FinishAttach
reason: Completes attach handshake; free is incidental, not a release interface.

name: AskPassword
reason: Initiates password prompt flow; alloc/free are internal to the operation.

name: PasswordProcessInput
reason: Processes typed password input; free/memset are incidental cleanup, not a release interface.

name: CreateTransTable
reason: Parses string and builds a translation table with calloc/malloc that persists; creates owned memory (FreeTransTable releases it). Returns int status, so allocator-leaning over non_memory.

name: realloc
reason: Reallocation wrapper returning a pointer to (re)allocated memory transferred to caller.

name: main
reason: Test driver; malloc/free used only as test scaffolding, not a memory interface.

name: RethinkDisplayViewports
reason: Recomputes display viewport layout; allocates and frees internal buffers as part of the operation, not an alloc/free interface.

name: MakeWindow
reason: Allocates a Window object (calloc), initializes it, adds to list and returns its identifier; creates an owned object.

cfr.name: MakeWindow
cfr.documentation: Umask & wlock are set for the user of the display, The display d (if specified) switches to that window.

name: FreeWindow
reason: Ends lifetime of whole Window object, releasing many nested resources (paster, pseudowin, acl, layers); interface is object destruction.

name: winexec
reason: Sets up and execs a program in a window; calloc/free are incidental to the exec operation, not the primary interface.

name: FreePseudowin
reason: Releases the pseudo-window resources/fields of the passed window object.

name: wmb_create
reason: Allocates and initializes a new WinMsgBuf and returns it to caller; documented as freed by wmb_free.

cfr.name: wmb_create
cfr.documentation: Allocate and initialize to the empty string a new window message buffer. The return value must be freed using wmbc_free.

name: wmb_expand
reason: Grows the buffer's owned memory via realloc to hold at least min bytes; acquires/extends dynamic memory.

cfr.name: wmb_expand
cfr.documentation: Attempts to expand the buffer to hold at least MIN bytes. The new size of the buffer is returned, which may be unchanged from the original size if additional memory could not be allocated.

name: wmb_free
reason: Frees memory allocated to the given window buffer; a fairly direct free wrapper for the buffer object.

cfr.name: wmb_free
cfr.documentation: Deinitialize and free memory allocated to the given window buffer

name: wmbc_create
reason: Allocates and initializes a buffer context and returns it; documented as freed by wmbc_free.

cfr.name: wmbc_create
cfr.documentation: Allocate and initialize a buffer context for the given buffer. The return value must be freed using wmbc_free.

name: wmbc_free
reason: Deinitializes and frees a previously allocated context passed in by the caller.

cfr.name: wmbc_free
cfr.documentation: Deinitializes and frees previously allocated context. The contained buffer must be freed separately; this function will not do so for you.
